mechanism_narrative: A declining trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing three months signals monetary easing, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an incipient recessionary labor-market turn, prompting equity risk repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger events, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC conditioned on trigger activation is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated and HOUST is simultaneously declining, tightening credit conditions are actively suppressing housing starts, indicating broader demand contraction that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month; the effect requires both conditions jointly, not either alone.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term contributes no incremental Rank IC over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign hit-rate falls below 50% over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS growth and a stable or falling UNRATE jointly indicate genuine labor-market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this signal reliably.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5% and UNRATE flat or declining
failure_condition: Joint-condition episodes fail to outperform single-factor PAYEMS-only model by any Rank IC margin over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, markets price in a higher probability of aggressive Fed tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during high-inflation trigger periods are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or Rank IC conditioned on trigger is below 0.03 across multiple inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS signals robust consumer demand during expansion regimes and predicts positive forward equity returns, but the same rise signals late-cycle overheating and predicts negative returns once FEDFUNDS is simultaneously elevated above 4.5%, causing the relationship's sign to flip across monetary regimes.
failure_condition: RSAFS-return correlation retains the same sign regardless of FEDFUNDS level across regimes, or Rank IC in the low-rate subsample is not significantly different from the high-rate subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 reflects durable real output expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive GDPC1 growth quarters show negative average forward returns.
